pages.full_text:
for pilgrims to view relics, reminiscent of catacombs. The church had an ambulatory crypt originally covered, now just looking liked a moat. Back along Brampton Valley Cyclepath. The blackberries are not good, but the path has a nice, distinctive design for its benches - lovely mosaics. We walked thro the tunnel & needed lights. 18m.
__Sunday 6th Sept__
 We went on another, 10m. bike ride, the other way, to Market Harborough. We met a pleasant Vietnamese & French couple on a bridge. The tunnel was worse. Good-looking swings on the outskirts of M.H. Lunch back at the van. Tinder pulled Simon off the bike.
  We set off for home just before 3 in case Naomi needed help after Walter's tongue-tie op.
pages.notes: Text begins mid-sentence as a continuation from the previous page — no handwritten page number visible. The year is not stated on this page; 'Sunday 6th Sept' matches 6 September 2015 calendrically, and the right-facing page shows entries from April and June 2016, so Sept 2015 is the most plausible year; confidence accordingly moderate. 'M.H.' is an abbreviation for Market Harborough. 'Tinder' appears to be a dog's name. 'Naomi' and 'Walter' are personal names; Walter's tongue-tie op provides a personal event anchor. The word 'liked' (as in 'looking liked a moat') is transcribed exactly as written — may be a slip for 'like'. The top portion of the text is the tail end of a prior entry (no date heading visible for it on this page).

pages.full_text:
April 2016
 We went for a walk along the canal in the Black Country with Pill, Nana, Peter, Eleni & Conrad. It was cold. We had tea in the van
__June 3rd Friday__
 Simon, Eve & Tinder left at about 3.30 Ombersley
 We reached [?B.rnary Ho] [?farmyard] campsite ** near Holt Fleet, Worcs. after about 40 min. £[?] per night for us all. lovely sunshine all evening. I took far too many cloths when we walked down to the R. Severn. A super walk. Old cart tracks. Views of banks of trees. We camped next to a fenced off pond/lake. Fishermen arrived at 7 the next morning. lots of them came & we saw one catch a big fish. It's a square field with loos & barn. 3 other vans/caravans.
 We left at 8.30. & bought a feast at Leggs including Bromyard Brie, which I just to my annoyance is actually made in Worcestershire.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 85 visible in top-right corner. Two separate diary entries on this page: (1) April 2016 — canal walk in the Black Country; (2) June 3rd Friday — camping trip near Holt Fleet, Worcs. 'June 3rd' was a Friday in 2016, confirming the year. The word 'Ombersley' appears to be written above or as a correction/annotation near the start of the June entry — it may be the destination or a crossed-out alternative campsite name. The campsite name is partially illegible: reads roughly as '[?B.rnary Ho] [?farmyard] campsite' — could be 'Barnary Ho[use]' or similar; 'Ombersley' as annotation may indicate Ombersley Court area. 'Pill', 'Nana', 'Peter', 'Eleni', 'Conrad', 'Simon', 'Eve', 'Tinder' are personal/family names (Tinder likely a dog). 'Bromyard Brie' is a cheese; Bromyard is a town in Herefordshire but diary writer notes it is made in Worcestershire. 'cloths' transcribed as written (possibly 'clothes'). Price per night is illegible. 'I just to my annoyance' transcribed verbatim — likely a slip for 'I find to my annoyance'.
